title: Welcome to MapleStory Worlds
author: MapleStory Worlds Creator Center
summary: A creator curriculum covering the MapleStory Worlds platform and editor, LuaScript fundamentals, client-server networking, building a sample world, publishing, and monetization.

sections.title: 1. Platform and editor orientation
items: Understand the MapleStory Worlds platform and the role of Maker.
Learn the client overview, navigation menu, Scene panel, Model List, Hierarchy, and Workspace.
Identify Layers, Scripts, and Resources in a creator project.

sections.title: 2. Basic concepts and LuaScript
items: Work with entities, components, properties, models, services, and logic.
Understand script types, script logic, script components, tables, variables, and functions.
Use the component model to attach behavior and data to objects in a world.

sections.title: 3. Networking in MapleStory Worlds
items: Learn property synchronization and function execution control across client and server contexts.
Separate local UI and input logic from shared or server-authoritative game logic.
Use the networking model to keep multiplayer state consistent.

sections.title: 4. Build the My Adventure sample
items: Design a map and establish the sample world's basic flow.
Add collectible coins, a monster encounter, and a finish flag.
Connect the objects and scripts into a playable beginning-to-end experience.

sections.title: 5. Publish and monetize
items: Create paid items and connect purchase logic and purchase components.
Review the finished world before publishing it to players.

notes: Original MPStorys curriculum outline based on the official Creator Center resource, verified on 2026-07-18. The source is a video curriculum, so MPStorys stores a structured outline rather than a transcript.
prerequisites: A creator project for following the sample exercises
steps: Learn the Maker interface and project hierarchy.
Practice entities, components, properties, and LuaScript fundamentals.
Separate client, server, UI, input, and synchronized logic.
Build the My Adventure sample with a map, coins, a monster, and a finish flag.
Add paid-item purchase and use logic, then prepare the world for publishing.
